sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 11.75 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.11)

statement.latex: Let us consider the class of groups with$n$generators and$m$relators. A~subclass of this class is called dense\/ if the ratio of the number of presentations of the form$< a_1, ,a_n R_1, ,R_m >$(where$|R_i|=d_i$) for groups from this subclass to the number of all such presentations converges to 1 when$d_1 + + d_m$tends to infinity. Prove that for every$k < m$and for any$n$the subclass of groups all...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
